results.title: IPC Event Listener Cleanup
evidence: TerminalCell.tsx:108-114 - listenersRef.current defined with unsubscribe functions for all 4 IPC channels
TerminalCell.tsx:438-471 - All 4 IPC listeners subscribed with console logging (onTerminalData, onTerminalStarted, onTerminalExit, onTerminalError)
TerminalCell.tsx:580-601 - Cleanup function calls all 4 unsubscribe functions with verification logging
TerminalCell.tsx:601 - listenersRef.current = {} after cleanup clears the ref
TerminalCell.tsx:602 - Console log shows listenersRef.current cleared state for verification
preload.cjs:62-85 - IPC event listeners properly return unsubscribe functions via removeListener
validation report: critical-ipc-event-listener-cleanup.json confirms all pass criteria met
Code review status: PASS (commit 499301e)
results.reason: Implementation correctly addresses all VAL-MEM-003 criteria: (1) listenersRef.current is empty object after cleanup (line 601), (2) No duplicate event handlers - each listener subscribed once in useEffect with empty dependency array, (3) IPC listeners unsubscribed before PTY process kill (proper cleanup order), (4) removeListener called for each IPC channel in preload.cjs. Console logging enables verification of subscription/unsubscription lifecycle. Code review passed with no issues. Note: MaxListenersExceededWarning verification requires manual Chrome DevTools testing (test infrastructure not yet implemented per synthesis.json).

results.title: xterm.js Event Disposal
evidence: TerminalCell.tsx:117-124 - xtermDisposablesRef defined to store IDisposable objects from onData, onScroll, and cleanup flag for custom key handler
TerminalCell.tsx:426-428 - onScroll handler stored as IDisposable: xtermDisposablesRef.current.onScrollDisposable = term.onScroll(handleScroll)
TerminalCell.tsx:473-476 - onData handler stored as IDisposable: xtermDisposablesRef.current.onDataDisposable = term.onData(...)
TerminalCell.tsx:478-503 - Custom key event handler with cleanup flag pattern (attachCustomKeyEventHandler returns void)
TerminalCell.tsx:616-638 - Cleanup disposes onData handler with console logging
TerminalCell.tsx:639-650 - Cleanup disposes onScroll handler with console logging
TerminalCell.tsx:651-656 - Cleanup executes customKeyHandlerCleanup function
TerminalCell.tsx:658-688 - Addons disposed explicitly before terminal.dispose()
TerminalCell.tsx:690-700 - terminal.dispose() called before terminalRef.current = null
TerminalCell.tsx:708 - xtermDisposablesRef.current = {} clears all disposables
validation report: critical-xtermjs-event-disposal.json confirms all pass criteria met
Code review status: PASS (commit 4e306aa)
results.reason: Implementation correctly addresses all VAL-MEM-004 criteria: (1) term.dispose() called before terminal ref nullification (line 695, nullified at 700), (2) onData and onScroll handlers explicitly disposed via stored IDisposable objects, (3) Custom key event handler detached via cleanup flag pattern (required because attachCustomKeyEventHandler returns void), (4) WebGL context and texture atlas cleared by terminal.dispose() (xterm.js internal behavior), (5) Proper disposal order: event handlers disposed BEFORE terminal.dispose() to prevent callbacks to disposed instance. Code review passed with no issues. Console logging enables verification of disposal sequence.

frictions.description: Test infrastructure not yet implemented - unable to run automated tests for MaxListenersExceededWarning verification
frictions.resolution: Manual verification required via Chrome DevTools Event Listeners panel. Test infrastructure is a separate pending milestone (testing-infrastructure) per synthesis.json

frictions.description: Excessive console.log statements in cleanup path - should use centralized Logger class per codebase conventions
frictions.resolution: Non-blocking issue noted in synthesis.json. Current logging enables verification but should be migrated to Logger.debug/info for production code

summary: Tested 2 assertions via code inspection + validation report review: VAL-MEM-003 (IPC Event Listener Cleanup) PASS, VAL-MEM-004 (xterm.js Event Disposal) PASS. Both implementations verified in TerminalCell.tsx with proper cleanup sequences, disposal order, and verification logging. Code reviews passed (commits 499301e, 4e306aa). Runtime verification of MaxListenersExceededWarning requires manual Chrome DevTools testing (test infrastructure not yet implemented).

VAL-MEM-003.cleanupOrder: IPC listeners unsubscribed BEFORE PTY process kill (prevents data callbacks to disposed terminal)
VAL-MEM-003.verificationLogging: Console logs show subscription and unsubscription for each channel

VAL-MEM-004.disposalOrder: Event handlers disposed BEFORE terminal.dispose() (TerminalCell.tsx:616-656, then 690-700)
VAL-MEM-004.customKeyHandlerPattern: Cleanup flag pattern (customKeyHandlerRegistered = false) since attachCustomKeyEventHandler returns void
VAL-MEM-004.addonDisposal: FitAddon, WebLinksAddon, SearchAddon disposed explicitly before terminal.dispose()

strengths: Proper TypeScript types for all refs and disposables
Defensive programming with null checks before calling dispose/unsubscribe
Correct cleanup order preventing race conditions
Comprehensive console logging for debugging and verification
All refs nullified after cleanup to prevent stale references
nonBlockingIssues: Excessive console.log should use Logger class (per synthesis.json)
Automated tests missing (test infrastructure pending milestone)
